analysis.analysis_was_performed_on: Portrait of Leo X with two cardinals by Raffaello Sanzio (Uffizi Galleries, Florence)

study_object_sample.taken_from: Portrait of Leo X with two cardinals by Raffaello Sanzio (Uffizi Galleries, Florence)
study_object_sample.taken_from_area: Cardinal robe — lower left corner sampling point

sample_preparation.preparation_notes: No physical sample preparation was required for the MA-XRF campaign, as the technique is fully non-invasive. The painting surface was visually inspected prior to scanning to identify accessible areas and define the scan grid. For the micro-fragment CS-LeoX-01, standard mounting in epoxy resin and polishing were performed before SEM-EDX analysis.
sample_preparation.used_method: Epoxy resin embedding and mechanical polishing
sample_preparation.used_protocol: OPD standard cross-section preparation protocol v2.1

analysis.analysis_title: Mapping with Macro X-ray Fluorescence Scanning of Raffaello's Portrait of Leo X
analysis.notes: MA-XRF analysis carried out on the Portrait of Leo X by Raffaello Sanzio, belonging to the collection of the Uffizi Galleries. The campaign aimed to study in detail the painting palette and technique employed by Raffaello, contextualising the results with other works by the artist. The analysis was performed before the painting's loan to the exhibition at the Scuderie del Quirinale in Rome for the 500th anniversary of the death of Raffaello (2020), during conservation treatments at the Opificio delle Pietre Dure, Florence. Several scans were performed for the almost full mapping of the painting; some areas could not be reached due to the dimensions and weight of the painting and the encumbrance of the easel.

took_place_at.place_name: Opificio delle Pietre Dure, Florence, Italy

agent.institution: Department of Physics and Astronomy, University of Florence / Florence Division, INFN/CHNet, Sesto Fiorentino, Florence, Italy
Department of Physics and Astronomy, University of Florence / Florence Division, INFN/CHNet, Sesto Fiorentino, Florence, Italy
Department of Physics and Astronomy, University of Florence / Florence Division, INFN/CHNet, Sesto Fiorentino, Florence, Italy
Department of Physics and Astronomy, University of Florence / Florence Division, INFN/CHNet, Sesto Fiorentino, Florence, Italy
Florence Division, INFN/CHNet, Sesto Fiorentino, Florence, Italy
Florence Division, INFN/CHNet, Sesto Fiorentino, Florence, Italy

used_method.method_notes: MA-XRF is a mapping technique derived from X-ray fluorescence (XRF) that provides elemental distribution maps across the surface of a painting in a non-invasive and non-destructive manner. It allows identification and spatial mapping of pigments and materials based on detected elements.

used_protocol.protocol_notes: The instrument was operated in situ at the Opificio delle Pietre Dure. Multiple overlapping scans were performed to achieve almost full mapping of the painting surface. A telemeter (Keyence IA-100) provided continuous control and adjustment of the sample–instrument distance during scanning.

maker.n: Cultural Heritage Network of the Italian National Institute of Nuclear Physics (INFN-CHNet)

component.model: Moxtek (40 kV maximum voltage, 0.1 mA maximum anode current, Mo anode)
Amptek XR100 SDD (25 mm² effective active surface, 500 µm thickness)
Physik Instrumente — 200 mm travel range in x and y directions, 50 mm stage along z

software.software_name: INFN-CHNet in-house MA-XRF instrument control and data analysis software
software.software_notes: In-house developed software used for instrument control and data analysis. Developed with contributions from Lara Palla and Caroline Czelusniak.
Open-source software for X-ray fluorescence data analysis, used for elemental map processing and spectral fitting.

dataset.dataset_title: INFN-CHNet reference XRF spectra library — pigments
